summary: Excellent soundboard recording of an all‑around stellar show highlighted by a smoking Help On The Way and a ripping Not Fade Away
review: Fans rave about this performance, calling the second set a pure joy from start to finish and singling out Help On The Way as one of Garcia’s top three ever. The soundboard source is described as crystal‑clear and “right on,” making the music come through with vivid detail.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are 5‑star, praising both the musical execution (exceptional second set, standout songs) and the audio fidelity. The show is deemed exceptional and the recording excellent, warranting a near‑top rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note the sound is “right on” with crisp, clear mix; no complaints about dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid, but the second set shines—continuous high energy from Help On The Way through a spacey Slipknot into an expansive Franklin’s Tower, capped by a rocking Saturday Night and a jubilant U.S. Blues encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia's lead on Help On The Way is repeatedly called one of his best performances.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh’s “signature opening bass lines” on Not Fade Away are praised for authority and power.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Samson drum solo is described as hot, with the drums driving much of the second‑set excitement.
